_rationale: PRIOR ITER USED name='Add' WHICH RETURNED EMPTY ON BOTH SIDES — green but content-free. Iter-7 audit: get_context requires either an object_id (from a prior search call) or a file+line+column triple; name-only lookup goes through an auto-search hint path that errors out before producing real context (Go returns {_auto_search_triggered:true, hint:...} when given name=X alone). object_id 'B' is deterministic for Go's `Add` function on the multi-lang corpus (verified via `search pattern=Add` — assigned by symbol_store in deterministic indexing order, identical across Go/C++). NEW C++ BUG SURFACED: get_context with id='B' returns Go non-empty (308 bytes including symbol_name, definition, purity block) but C++ returns empty {"contexts":[],"count":0}. Probe: /tmp/parity-iter4/get_context_Add.{go,cpp}.json — Go 308 bytes, C++ 25 bytes. Filed as iter-7 fix subtask. Until C++ implements get_context-by-id lookup, text body stays in ignore tier; envelope still locked. When C++ fix lands, flip rationale and move text to stable.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope only. Both sides return a content[].type='text' frame confirming the tool dispatches and produces a JSON-encoded body — even when the body itself diverges (Go: contexts populated, C++: empty). Tighter parity blocked on C++ get_context-by-id implementation.
tiers._rationale_ignore: result.content[].text diverges because C++ get_context-by-id returns empty contexts where Go returns the resolved symbol. KARPATHY-RULE-6: this is NOT a silent fallback — bug is filed as a fix subtask and text-body parity will tighten once C++ matches Go's id-resolution path.
